role_title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage

level_signals: Desire to do individual contributor product management work
Comp range $194K–$239K base, consistent with senior/staff IC at Airbnb
Responsible for homepage and discovery across all key business verticals — high-scope ownership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience creating product messaging and delivering to customers and the media.
Demonstrated track record of building cross-functional and executive leadership alignment.
Experience working on consumer products with marketplace dynamic on a global scale.
Entrepreneurial track record of taking an idea to reality.
A strong ability to effectively use data and perform business analysis to support product strategy/decisions.
Excited to drive execution across teams in a fast-paced environment.
Desire to do individual contributor product management work.

explicitly_excluded: Alaska, Indiana, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Alabama, Mississippi, Oklahoma, Delaware, Rhode Island

scope.surface: Airbnb Homepage and Guest Discovery (across Homes, Services, Experiences, Hotels, Luxe, Partnerships)
scope.business_model: B2C marketplace (two-sided: guests and hosts), global scale
primary_metrics_inferred: Lifecycle campaign performance tied to discovery
core_responsibilities: Lead the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for the discovery needs of all key business verticals (e.g. Homes, Services, Experiences, International) and new bets (e.g. Hotels, Luxe, Partnerships)
Lead the development of new discovery features and content formats that drive core guest and bookings growth.
Incorporate the latest AI technologies and relevance models to uplevel Airbnb's browsing experience
Collaborate with marketing teams to extend Airbnb's discovery strategy to our lifecycle and growth marketing campaigns.
Craft the product narrative and marketing strategies that communicate the benefits and features of these products both internally and externally.

Develop product roadmaps and build alignment across product teams (Tech, Design), cross-functional partners (FP&A, Marketing, Global Operations, Customer Experience, Trust, etc.) and Leadership/Executive team.
Inspire teams, leadership, and the company to support the success of your products and business outcomes.
Build deep expertise on the subject matter both through user research, marketplace data, and an understanding of the competitive landscape.
Define goals and success with clearly measurable objectives and key results.
Efficiently execute to ship products and deliver impact for our business and users.

compensation.equity_form: bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits mentioned

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($194K–$239K base) plus equity and bonus
Clear and specific product surface — homepage and discovery for all guest verticals
High-visibility, high-impact role spanning the full Airbnb product breadth
IC PM role with senior scope — explicitly states 'desire to do individual contributor product management work'
Cross-functional remit is well-defined (engineering, design, marketing, data, ops, trust)
red_flags: Requires physical presence in SF, LA, or NYC — no remote option; large exclusion list of US states
Visa sponsorship policy not stated — unclear for international candidates
JD does not specify educational requirements — may be inferred but not explicitly stated
Title says 'Product Manager' but the scope and comp suggest a Staff/Senior PM level; title may underrepresent the seniority expected

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years required + scope breadth)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of Airbnb marketplace)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
location.visa_sponsorship (not stated in JD; marked unclear)
location.office_time_required (not stated; only city requirement specified)
ambiguities: Role requires being based in SF, LA, or NYC but does not explicitly state full-time in-office vs hybrid — location.type set to 'onsite' based on language 'need to be based in' with no hybrid qualifier mentioned
No nice-to-haves section exists in the JD; all requirements are listed under 'Your Expertise' as unconditional bullets — all treated as hard must-haves
